questions.assertion: Assertion (A): Index numbers are used to express the weighted average of the price relatives of a basket of commodities.
questions.reason: Reason (R): Index numbers are calculated by comparing the prices of a basket of commodities at different points of time.

(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: This is a correct representation of the concept of index numbers.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): The cost of living index is a type of consumer price index.
questions.reason: Reason (R): It measures the changes in the prices of goods and services consumed by a typical urban household.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: This is an accurate statement about cost of living index.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): The law of indices states that the product of the indices of two variables is equal to the index of their product.
questions.reason: Reason (R): It helps in calculating the index of a composite commodity.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: This is a correct application of the law of indices in index number theory.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): The Fisher ideal index is a type of price index.
questions.reason: Reason (R): It is a geometric mean of the Laspeyres and Paasche price indices.

(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): The index number formula for a single commodity is given by: I = (Σp1q1 + Σp2q2) / (Σp0q0 + Σp0q0).
